summary: The proposed coverage assessment preserves the cellular_senescence/senolytics scope and keeps active registry surveillance, review-landscape gaps, source-snapshot limitations, and extraction-grade evidence boundaries in separate taxonomy categories. No open taxonomy_mapping blocker was found.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed coverage_assessment remains scoped to hallmark_id cellular_senescence and track_id senolytics while naming fisetin and D+Q registry surveillance as senolytics track follow-up.
findings.why_it_matters: This preserves the intended intervention-class and hallmark boundary and avoids drifting the repair into unrelated clinical or mechanistic taxonomies.
findings.recommended_action: No taxonomy change required; keep future registry surveillance records under the senolytics track only when source snapshots support the specific registry status.

findings.claim_or_issue: Deferred fisetin frailty registries NCT03430037 and NCT03675724 are described as active-trials registry-surveillance gaps rather than added to covered_source_ids, covered_finding_ids, or extraction-grade result records.
findings.why_it_matters: The taxonomy distinguishes candidate registry surveillance work from extracted efficacy, safety, or mechanistic evidence, preventing unsupported elevation of unfetched registries.
findings.recommended_action: No change required; create no-results or registry-status records only after retained ClinicalTrials.gov source snapshots are available.

findings.claim_or_issue: The coverage_assessment separates active_trials_registries, recent_reviews_meta_analyses, taxonomy_boundary, safety_durability, and human_interventional categories instead of merging registry status with clinical effect claims.
findings.why_it_matters: Separate categories keep coverage gaps, evidence maturity, and synthesis readiness auditable for the senolytics track.
findings.recommended_action: No change required; maintain these category boundaries in any later surveillance or extraction-refresh candidate.
